title: oxalis procumbens subsp. bathieana
intro: Oxalis procumbens subsp. bathieana, also known as the common woodsorrel, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family and is native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties due to its high oxalic acid and anthocyanin content. The health benefits of Oxalis procumbens subsp. bathieana include its potential to help manage conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Oxalis procumbens subsp. bathieana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
